oai_dc:dc.dc:title: Good records protect you and your practice
oai_dc:dc.dc:creator: Navarre, Christine
oai_dc:dc.dc:subject: veterinary medical records
liability
standard of care
oai_dc:dc.dc:description: High-quality medical records are a cornerstone of veterinary practice, protecting both patients and practitioners. Incomplete or inconsistent documentation increases liability risk, impairs workflow efficiency and undermines patient care. This paper discusses the legal, ethical and practical importance of vet­erinary records, the concept of decision fatigue and its impact on recordkeeping, and how veterinarians can meet or exceed the standard of care. Strategies are provided for developing ef­fective documentation systems, managing liability exposure, maintaining professional integrity and preparing for interac­tions with attorneys, investigators and expert witnesses.
oai_dc:dc.dc:source: American Association of Bovine Practitioners Conference Proceedings; Vol. 58 No. 2 (2025): 2025 AABP Annual Conference Proceedings; 156-157
oai_dc:dc.dc:rights: Copyright (c) 2026 American Association of Bovine Practitioners
